user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate PWM functionality to the AURIX TC4D7 target on KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Use eGTM Cluster 0 ATOM0 CH0–2 to generate three complementary, center-aligned PWM pairs at 20 kHz with 1 μs deadtime (U,V,W), and ATOM0 CH3 as a 20 kHz falling-edge 50% duty trigger routed to TMADC0. TMADC0: use channels CH0–CH4 mapped to pins P40.0, P40.1, P40.2, P40.3, P40.4, respectively; queue conversion order [CH0, CH1, CH2, CH3, CH4] per PWM period; results polled each trigger. Replace IfxScuWdt with IfxWtu. Update U,V,W duties every 500 ms and toggle LED P03.9.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Adopt the unified high-level IfxEgtm_Pwm driver on TC4xx with eGTM CMU CLK0 at 100 MHz for 20 kHz center-aligned PWM and HW dead-time via DTM. Configure ATOM0 CH0–2 as a synchronized 3-phase inverter group with complementary outputs and 1 µs deadtime. Configure ATOM0 CH3 to output a 50% duty, falling-edge marker and route it to TMADC0 with IfxEgtm_Trigger_trigToAdc. Initialize TMADC0 with 5 queued channels and poll results each trigger. Replace watchdog handling with IfxWtu. Consolidate into egtm_atom_3ph_pwm.c/.h and call periodic duty updates from main while toggling P03.9.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: IfxEgtm_Pwm is a unified, high-level PWM driver available for TC4xx that supports synchronized multi-channel PWM, complementary pairs, center alignment, and dead-time management—ideal for 3-phase inverter PWM and synchronized trigger generation.

initialization_apis: Consult TC4xx iLLD to confirm API names and signatures

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, update U,V,W PWM duties (initially 25%, 50%, 75%) and toggle LED P03.9; for each PWM period, poll TMADC0 results (5 channels) triggered by EGTM ATOM0 CH3.

driver_changes: Consult TC3xx→TC4xx migration guide or iLLD release notes to confirm driver and API mappings

api_changes.reason: Use TC4xx synced start API for channel group
Initialize WTU watchdog per TC4xx; use configuration to keep watchdog inactive as required
Initialize system watchdog per TC4xx; keep inactive as required

migration_notes.context_preserved: The solution configures eGTM Cluster 0 ATOM0 for three center-aligned, complementary PWM pairs with 1 µs deadtime at 20 kHz using IfxEgtm_Pwm. ATOM0 CH3 generates a 50% waveform whose falling edge is used as a 20 kHz TMADC0 trigger through IfxEgtm_Trigger_trigToAdc; TMADC0 runs 5 queued channels in polled mode. Duties are updated in the main loop every 500 ms and the LED on P03.9 is toggled. IfxScuWdt usage is migrated to IfxWtu. Pins P00.x/P33.0 from the original request are adapted to template-verified EGTM ATOM0 TOUT pins on P02.x (U/V/W: P02.0/1, P02.2/3, P02.4/5; trigger: P02.6) to keep functionality on the provided target template. Original request referenced PWM pins on P00.x/P33.0; on KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE the eGTM ATOM0 TOUT signals are routed to Port 02 and validated in the template. Mapping is therefore: U: P02.0 (TOUT0) high / P02.1 (TOUT1) low; V: P02.2 (TOUT2) high / P02.3 (TOUT3) low; W: P02.4 (TOUT4) high / P02.5 (TOUT5) low; ADC trigger: P02.6 (TOUT6). These P02.x pins are available on the board connectors and are functionally equivalent for ATOM0 PWM/trigger usage, ensuring identical timing, polarity control, and routability on KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE.

PWM.rationale: … Available in template | … Matches target family (TC4xx) | Complexity: advanced | Best for: 3-phase inverter, motor control, multi-channel synchronized PWM | Features: multi_channel_sync, complementary_pairs, dead_time, center_aligned, edge_aligned
